paper_title: Fluid Inclusion Isotope Measurements and Analysis

methods.description: Process for measuring water isotopes in fluid inclusions using a crusher device, humidity generator, and spectrometer to analyze stalagmite samples.

steps.description: Samples were measured with all analytical components under constant humid conditions to avoid memory effects.
steps.searchable_summary: measure samples under constant humid conditions

steps.description: Samples were loaded into the pre-heated crusher device and approximately 15-25 min were needed for background stabilization.
steps.searchable_summary: stabilize background in pre-heated crusher device

steps.description: Raw δ 18 O and δ 2 H data for sample and background were corrected for the humidity dependency of the analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: correct isotope data for humidity dependency
inputs: raw δ 18 O and δ 2 H data

steps.description: Sample δ 18 O and δ 2 H values were then calculated following the algorithm presented by Affolter et al. (2014).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate isotope values using Affolter algorithm
outputs: calculated δ 18 O and δ 2 H values

steps.description: The accuracy of water isotope measurements with the crushing line was assessed with standard waters sealed in borosilicate glass capillaries.
steps.searchable_summary: validate isotope measurements with standard waters

methods.method_name: Relationship of Fluid Inclusion Isotope Data to the LMWL
methods.description: Analysis of the relationship between fluid inclusion isotope data and the Local Meteoric Water Line (LMWL) to identify deviations and trends.

steps.description: A cross-plot of the stalagmite fluid inclusion water isotope data shows that the majority of samples have values outside 1σ analytical uncertainty of the LMWL.
steps.searchable_summary: create cross-plot of isotope data against LMWL

steps.description: Calculated the slopes in δ 2 H-δ 18 O for samples with three or more replicate measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate slopes in δ 2 H-δ 18 O for replicates

methods.method_name: Comparison of Inclusion Isotope Data With Independent Temperature and Dripwater δ 18 O Constraints
methods.description: Comparison of fluid inclusion isotope data with independent temperature and dripwater δ 18 O data to assess offsets and validate results.

steps.description: Compare fluid inclusion isotope results to independent information on temperature and dripwater δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: compare isotope results with temperature and dripwater data

steps.description: Use fluid-calcite δ 18 O temperatures to validate against microthermometry estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: validate fluid-calcite δ 18 O temperatures with microthermometry

methods.description: Use a Rayleigh model to simulate isotopic composition changes during evaporation and compare with expected cave conditions.

steps.description: Use a Rayleigh model to simulate the isotopic composition of water during fluid inclusion analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate isotopic composition using Rayleigh model

steps.description: Calculate equilibrium fractionation factors at 120°C using relationships from Horita and Wesolowski (1994).
